body.title: CLOSED: TWM effective salience decays with age at read time using exponential half-life
body.result: exp(-age_s/1800) multiplier applied at twm_read() before lateral inhibition; stored salience unchanged
body.description:
Items in TWM hold full salience until TTL expiry. Biological working memory uses dopamine-gated decay: items that have been in WM longer gradually lose attentional weight. A stale TWM item at salience 0.8 unaddressed for 30 minutes should not compete equally with a fresh 0.8 item.

In cortex.twm_read(), compute effective_salience = stored_salience * exp(-age_seconds / 1800.0) where age_seconds = NOW - created_at and half_life = 1800s (30 min). At 30min: multiplier = 0.37; at 60min: 0.14. Return effective_salience for ordering and filtering. Stored salience unchanged — decay is read-time only.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (MEDIUM inertia)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia); theigors/rules/database (read-time computation, no stored mutation)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: age-decay multiplier applied at read time using created_at timestamp already on TWM rows. Out of scope: changing stored salience; changes to TTL mechanism.
**Test plan:** A TWM item written 30 min ago at salience 0.8 reads back with effective_salience ≈ 0.30 (0.8 * 0.37). A fresh item at salience 0.8 reads back at 0.80. Dashboard TWM panel shows decayed values. Existing TTL-based expiry unchanged.
